title: Diporidium wightianum
intro: Diporidium wightianum, also known as "Kalmegh" or "Kutki," is a traditional medicinal plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, which may aid in treating various digestive issues and liver disorders. The bioactive constituents of Diporidium wightianum include coumarins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tablets, capsules, and decoctions, which are used to treat conditions like jaundice, fever, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Diporidium wightianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Diporidium.
